page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong performance characteristics, particularly in compute-heavy and instruction-set-dependent tasks. The Intel Xeon processor features advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, which contribute to a strong Passmark CPU score of 66,066.16 and an extended instructions test score of 59,793.20. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier performance levels at 36.1 GB/sec and 15.17 GB/sec, respectively. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 3,532.24 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48ls_v7 offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint. By minimizing memory allocation to 2.0 GB per vCPU, it reduces resource overhead for workloads that do not require massive RAM arrays. This makes it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound tasks. The instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, achieving 75.36 GB/sec static web serving throughput, Redis database operations at 79.02 million operations per second, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference, where it demonstrates strong prompt processing and text generation speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores and is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 66,066.16 and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. It also demonstrates strong performance in static web serving and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks. This server is highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory allocations, making it suitable for web serving, high-throughput Redis operations, and compute-heavy batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, delivering strong performance for CPU-bound workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-density Intel Xeon processing and a balanced low-memory configuration for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
Low-memory configuration with 96.0 GB RAM providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression performance
Strong capabilities in static web serving and Redis database operations
Optimized for CPU-bound workloads without local storage requirements
